checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=540557620.07 (end 2009-01-31) growing to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=868383287.83 (end 2010-01-30), a +60.6% increase, while Revenues=7181189247.85 (FY2009, 2008-02-03 to 2009-01-31) fell to Revenues=6833452360.01 (FY2010, 2009-02-01 to 2010-01-30), a -4.8% decline

AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=803745446.15 (end 2013-02-02) growing to AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=1103735320.73 (end 2014-02-01), a +37.3% increase, while Revenues=7711928769.66 (FY2013) grew to Revenues=8285754506.98 (FY2014), only +7.4%

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?
Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=1774357187.32 (FY2013, 2012-01-29 to 2013-02-02) versus NetIncomeLoss=746177900.81 (FY2013) — OCF consistently exceeds NI across the series, e.g. NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=2904901222.03 (FY2011) vs NetIncomeLoss=2200502566.28 (FY2011)

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: InventoryNet=597380411.3 (end 2011-01-29) grew to InventoryNet=861867906.31 (end 2012-01-28), +44.3%, while Revenues fell from Revenues=8790758636.9 (FY2011) to Revenues=8258106119.24 (FY2012), -6.1%

InventoryNet=609481448.61 (end 2013-02-02) grew to InventoryNet=846636954.69 (end 2014-02-01), +38.9%, while Revenues grew only from Revenues=7711928769.66 (FY2013) to Revenues=8285754506.98 (FY2014), +7.4%

Gross margin computed from CostOfGoodsSold=3585708696.25 (FY2011) / Revenues=8790758636.9 = 59.2% declined to CostOfGoodsSold=4487279784.7 (FY2015) / Revenues=9022143515.58 = 50.3%, a persistent multi-year margin compression coinciding with inventory buildup

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=-271268164.75 (quarterly, 2009-02-01 to 2009-05-02) followed by NetIncomeLoss=142362424.62 and NetIncomeLoss=490659094.95 in subsequent quarters, showing large seasonal swings consistent with the retail holiday cycle seen in Revenues quarterly figures (e.g. Revenues=1269085335.33 vs Revenues=2050592813.28)

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=17927642.96 (end 2011-01-29) declined sharply to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=6481307.79 (end 2012-01-28), a -63.8% drop, while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent only declined from 1118119297.1 to 991211737.1 (-11.4%), reducing reserve coverage from 1.60% to 0.65% of receivables

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.quote: NT 10-Q filed 2006-09-07, NT 10-Q/A filed 2006-09-28, NT 10-Q filed 2006-12-06, NT 10-K filed 2007-03-29, NT 10-Q filed 2007-06-08 — a cluster of late-filing notifications over roughly nine months
10-K filed 2007-07-02 followed within days by 10-K/A filed 2007-07-13, and 10-Q filed 2007-07-02 with 10-Q/A also filed 2007-07-02, indicating rapid restatement/correction activity

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly and annual data points span from fiscal year end 2009-01-31 through 2015-05-02 across Revenues, AccountsReceivableNetCurrent, InventoryNet, NetIncomeLoss, and NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities, providing roughly 6-7 years of comparable trend data

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Potential premature revenue recognition or extension of customer/store-credit financing terms that inflates receivables ahead of matching revenue growth, most visible in FY2010 (AR +60.6% vs revenue -4.8%) and FY2014 (AR +37.3% vs revenue +7.4%).

rationale_evidence.quote: AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=868383287.83 (2010-01-30) vs AccountsReceivableNetCurrent=540557620.07 (2009-01-31); Revenues=6833452360.01 (FY2010) vs Revenues=7181189247.85 (FY2009)

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible delayed recognition of inventory write-downs/obsolescence reserves as inventory balances grew substantially faster than revenue while gross margin compressed steadily (59.2%->50.3% FY2011-FY2015), which would overstate inventory and gross profit.

rationale_evidence.quote: CostOfGoodsSold=3585708696.25 (FY2011)/Revenues=8790758636.9 vs CostOfGoodsSold=4487279784.7 (FY2015)/Revenues=9022143515.58 shows margin decline from 59.2% to 50.3%

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Reduction of bad-debt allowance/reserve coverage (from 1.60% to 0.65% of AR) despite still-large receivables balances could understate the allowance for doubtful accounts, thereby overstating net receivables and net income.

rationale_evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=17927642.96 (2011-01-29) declined to AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=6481307.79 (2012-01-28) while AccountsReceivableNetCurrent only fell from 1118119297.1 to 991211737.1

top_signals: AR growth materially outpaced revenue growth in FY2010 (+60.6% vs -4.8%) and FY2014 (+37.3% vs +7.4%)
Inventory growth materially outpaced revenue growth in FY2012 (+44.3% vs -6.1%) and FY2014 (+38.9% vs +7.4%), coinciding with multi-year gross margin compression (59.2%->50.3%)
Allowance for doubtful accounts coverage ratio fell sharply from 1.60% to 0.65% of receivables between FY2011 and FY2012
Historical cluster of NT 10-Q/NT 10-K filings and rapid 10-K/A and 10-Q/A amendments in 2006-2007 indicating past filing control issues
Operating cash flow consistently and comfortably exceeded net income across all years, which is a mitigating (positive) quality-of-earnings signal
